ccel.title: NPNF-213. Gregory the Great (II), Ephraim Syrus, Aphrahat

title: To Eulogius, Patriarch of Alexandria.

source.title: NPNF-213. Gregory the Great (II), Ephraim Syrus, Aphrahat

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: I have received at the hands of the bearer of these presents the letter of your most sweet Holiness, speaking to me about your cause being terminated speedily.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: But, as soon as he had come, he learnt how the possession which he sought from our Church was held, and soon satisfied himself about it.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: The business he had with others he settled without contention.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: But concerning the matter which ought by all means to have been written about to me, your Holiness has written nothing, considering me also to be tardy therein.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: And indeed, for fear of its breaking out into the scandal of division, I have been unwilling to be the author of such division.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: For I have chosen that whatever may follow should ensue through others.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: But in time to come, God granting it, you will have proof that in a cause wherein I desire to please God I am not afraid of men.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: Concerning this I took care to write to you before now, even when you went to Constantinople.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: As to the timber, I had prepared pieces of a larger size, as your Blessedness had requested in your letter; but so small a ship has been sent here that it could not carry them, unless they had been cut.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: But I was unwilling to have them cut, and have reserved for your judgment what should be done about them.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: If you do not require them, we will adapt them for other uses here.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: Moreover, I beg of your Holiness to pray for me earnestly, since I am incessantly pressed down by pains of gout, and swords of barbarians, and distressing cares.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: But, if you bestow on me the help of your prayer, I believe that you will strongly aid me against all adversities.
